⚠ Missouri no hands laws — pull over to use
⚙️ Settings
Tap any panel to expand. All changes save when you tap Save & Close.
📊 AI Usage (this session)
Token counts for requests made with your key. Session resets on refresh; Today, 7d, and 30d are persisted on-device.
No AI calls yet this session.
🔑 AI Provider Keys
AI features need an API key. Set up Gemini first — it's free for typical inspector use. Add Anthropic as a paid fallback if you want unlimited capacity. One PIN unlocks all your keys.
⚡ Google Gemini FREE
Not set
📖 How to get a free Gemini key (2 min)
1. Go to aistudio.google.com/app/apikey
2. Sign in with your Google account (gmail works)
3. Tap "Create API key"
4. Choose "Create API key in new project" (or use an existing one)
5. Copy the AIza… string
6. Paste it above with your PIN

Free tier: ~500 requests/day on gemini-2.5-flash. That's enough for ~50 photo scans plus voice cleanup, route narrative, and address validation every day, indefinitely. No credit card required.
Privacy: The free tier may use your prompts to improve Google's models. For sensitive inspection data, use the paid tier (still cheap) or Anthropic instead.
🛡️ Anthropic Claude PAID
Not set
📖 How to get an Anthropic key
1. Go to console.anthropic.com
2. Sign up — account is free, but to use the key you must prepay $5 (Settings → Billing → Buy credits)
3. Tap "API Keys" → "Create Key" → copy the sk-ant-… string
4. Paste it above with your PIN

Cost: ~$0.004 per photo scan on Claude Haiku 4.5. $5 covers ~1,250 scans = roughly 4 months of heavy daily use.
Privacy: Anthropic does not train on API traffic by default. Cleaner choice for sensitive inspection data.
🚧 OpenAI / GPT UNAVAILABLE
Not currently compatible with CityRoute's no-server architecture — OpenAI doesn't reliably support direct browser API calls (CORS). The technology is evolving fast; if OpenAI enables browser-direct access, support will be added.
🔒 ENCRYPTION PIN (UNLOCKS ALL KEYS)
One PIN protects all your keys — you'll enter it once per session. Don't lose it.
🎯 AI Provider Priority
Set the order CityRoute tries your providers. The recommended setup is Gemini → Anthropic — try the free option first, fall back to Anthropic only when Gemini errors or rate-limits. You stay free until you actually exceed Gemini's quota.
DEFAULT PRIORITY (ALL FEATURES)
PER-FEATURE OVERRIDES
Override the default for specific features. Useful if (e.g.) you want photo scans on Anthropic only.
📱 App Install & GPS
Checking…
GPS works after installing
📞 Contacts for Follow-up
CSB is always shown first. Select additional departments to include.
+ ADD CUSTOM DEPARTMENT
🏷️ Custom Stop Types
Added from the stop type dropdown on the add-stop panel. Max 10. Deleting reverts stops to "Other".
🛠 Diagnostics
When something silently doesn't work, recent absorbed errors may show up here. The buffer holds the most recent 100 entries and clears on app reload.
Tap REFRESH to view recent absorbed errors.
⏰ Fiscal-Day Cutoff & Archive
Once a fiscal day rolls over, completed stops from earlier days move out of your active list and into the archive. Pending stops stay in the list. Set the cutoff to whatever marks the END of your work day — anything completed AFTER this time is treated as the next fiscal day's work.
Archive empty.
📈 Google Sheets Sync
Paste the Apps Script Web App URL to enable "Push to Sheets" from the Data tab. One-time setup — follow steps below.
Not configured
📋 Setup steps + Apps Script code
1. Open a new Google Sheet at sheets.google.com
2. Click Extensions → Apps Script
3. Delete the existing code and paste the script below
4. Click Deploy → New deployment → Web app
5. Set "Who has access" to Anyone
6. Click Deploy and copy the Web App URL
7. Paste the URL above and tap SAVE URL 
function doPost(e) {
  var data = JSON.parse(e.postData.contents);
  var ss = SpreadsheetApp.getActiveSpreadsheet();
  var sh = ss.getSheetByName('Stops') || ss.insertSheet('Stops');
  if (sh.getLastRow() === 0)
    sh.appendRow(['Date','Address','Type','Notes','Done','DurationMins','Lat','Lng','Inspector']);
  (data.stops || []).forEach(s => sh.appendRow([
    data.exportedAt, s.address, s.type,
    s.notes||'', s.done, s.durationMins||'',
    s.lat||'', s.lng||'', data.inspector
  ]));
  return ContentService.createTextOutput('ok');
}
🔘 Header Buttons
Show or hide the clock-in icon in the header. Off by default.
🙏 Help Improve the App
Opt in to share anonymous usage pings with the developer. Pings include: install status, app open events, and which features you use. No names, addresses, or photos are ever sent. Off by default.
You can turn this off at any time. Each ping is a single tiny POST to a Google Apps Script, then discarded.
📧 How email attachments work

Short version: the app keeps a temporary 48-hour cache of stamped photos so the 📧 Email + Photos button can attach them to email automatically. After 48 hours the cached copies are deleted to keep the app small. Your phone gallery always keeps the permanent copies (those are saved by Download / Share, never the cache).

Why a cache and not unlimited storage? Web apps (which is what this is, technically — a PWA) live in a sandbox the browser enforces. The sandbox lets the app hand photos to your phone (via the Share sheet or Download) but never lets it read back photos from your gallery. So once a photo leaves the cache, the app can\u2019t pull it back to attach. Storing them indefinitely in-app would slowly fill your phone\u2019s storage and on iOS would risk being silently wiped by the system. 48 hours is the sweet spot — long enough to cover Friday\u2192Monday workflows, short enough to stay tiny.

What to do in each case:

  • 📷 Just took photos: tap 📧 Email Summary + Photos in the photo preview, OR mark the stop done and tap 📧📷 Email Summary + photos attached in the close-stop screen. Either way your share sheet opens with the stop summary text and all the stamped photos pre-attached.
  • 📅 Within 48 hours: open the stop, tap ✅ MARK COMPLETE (or just tap the existing complete stop), the close-stop modal will show the 📧📷 Email Summary + photos attached button if cached photos still exist for this stop. The cache count appears under the button.
  • 📝 Older than 48 hours: the 📧 Email Stop Summary button still sends the full text summary, and the email body lists the exact filenames of every photo taken at that stop — so you (or the recipient) know which files to attach manually from the gallery.
  • 📱 Filenames: photos are named like 2801daltonavenue042326101832847.jpg (address + date + time + ms). Search for that filename in your gallery or file manager.

Cache size limits: normally 200MB. On phones with less than ~150MB free, the cap drops to 50MB to be polite. Either way, when full, the oldest photos go first. The newest photo for any stop is always preserved as long as it fits.

The cache is a working convenience, not a permanent record. The photos you see in your phone\u2019s Photos app or Downloads folder are the real archive — the app never modifies or removes those.

Upload your city ID card. Use rotation buttons to orient correctly.

✨ Nearby-Stops Highlight Radius
When two or more pending stops sit within this radius, each of them gets a gold border in the Stops, Route, and Map tabs — so you can spot natural clusters at a glance.
Default: 2 blocks. Set higher to see more matches; lower for only the closest pairs. Off turns off the gold-border highlighting entirely.
📷 Photo Cache (48h)
A temporary in-app copy of every photo the app captures — stamped photos, photoscan camera images, and custom-layer scan photos all share this pool. The total respects the same size cap and auto-deletes after 48 hours. Your phone gallery keeps permanent copies regardless.
Loading…
When ON, the camera image you took before AI extracted addresses is offered through your phone's share sheet (save to gallery, send to Drive, etc). Off by default for privacy and storage.
📷 Photo Stamp
What gets burned into the corner of every stamped photo.
🏷️ Position
Choosing a position pre-fills title, division, department, scope, and the matching contacts — and sets defaults for Roles, Summary & Ordinance pages.
💾 Profile Backup & Restore
Saves the currently-active profile to a single file you can email yourself or stash in cloud storage. When you get a new phone, restore the file to bring all your stops, settings, ID card, custom map layers, and history with you.
Included: stops, route order, settings, ID photo, custom stop types, recipients, custom map layers (geocoded addresses), photo log filenames, daily metrics history.
Excluded: photo bytes (kept on your phone storage), encrypted API key (re-enter on the new device), session-only flags.
👤 Profiles
Use multiple profiles when one phone is shared between job roles or inspectors. Each profile has its own stops, ID photo, settings, and API key. Switching profiles reloads the app.
A new profile starts empty — the setup wizard runs again so you can configure it. Existing profiles are not affected.
🎤 Voice Note Cleanup
When you dictate a stop note via the mic button, run the raw transcript through Claude to fix punctuation, expand spoken numbers, and correct mishearings of common St. Louis street names. Original raw text is kept so you can undo.
Off by default. Requires an AI key (Gemini free or Anthropic paid). Cost is negligible — typically < 1¢ per note on Anthropic; covered by Gemini's free tier.
⚠ Danger Zone
RESET erases every piece of data this app has stored on your phone — stops, route history, custom map layers, completed metrics, settings, ID photo, encrypted API key, everything. Next time you open the app the setup wizard will run from scratch.

Export your profile first if you want any of this data back. There is no undo.
📥 Import Profile
Reading file…
As a new profile (recommended)
Creates a new profile with the file's data. Your current profile is not changed. Switches to the new profile after import.
Or merge into current profile
Adds stops, archives, transactions, custom types, and map layers from the file. Inspector identity, ID photo, API key, preferences, and webhook URLs are kept as-is on this device (additive only — never overwritten).

✨ Supercharge with AI
Add an AI key to unlock smart features that work right on your phone:
📷 Photo address scan — snap a printed route sheet, AI reads the addresses
🎤 Voice note cleanup — dictate a stop note, AI fixes punctuation + St. Louis street names
🌅 End-of-day debrief — patterns, hot-spots, tomorrow's priorities
💬 Ask your data — natural-language questions about your stops
🎨 Custom layer scan — photograph a list, build a map layer from it
⚡ Recommended: Google Gemini FREE
Most inspectors will never need to pay. Gemini's free tier (~500 requests/day) covers normal field use indefinitely — photo scans, voice cleanup, and route narratives all included. No credit card.
📖 Get a free Gemini key (2 minutes)
1. Go to aistudio.google.com/app/apikey
2. Sign in with your Google account (any gmail works)
3. Tap "Create API key"
4. Choose "Create API key in new project"
5. Copy the AIza… string
6. Paste it below
🛡️ Add Anthropic too (paid fallback) OPTIONAL
Anthropic Claude is the paid fallback used only if Gemini errors or rate-limits. Most users won't need this. Adding it gives you unlimited capacity and a privacy-friendlier option for sensitive data — Anthropic doesn't train on API traffic by default. Cost: ~$0.004/photo scan; $5 covers ~1,250 scans.
📖 Get an Anthropic key
1. Visit console.anthropic.com
2. Sign up — free account, but you must prepay $5 (Settings → Billing → Buy credits)
3. "API Keys" → "Create Key" → copy the sk-ant-… string
4. Paste below
One PIN encrypts all your keys. The unencrypted key never leaves your phone except when calling the AI provider. Don't lose the PIN — there's no recovery.

St. Louis City Employment Opportunities ↗
🗺️
STLCityRoute
FIELD WORKER GUIDE
🧭 What is STLCityRoute?
STLCityRoute is a mobile route planner built for field workers who visit multiple addresses in a single shift — city inspectors, code enforcement officers, pest control techs, utility workers, property managers, and anyone else working a stop list.

No login. No server. All data stays on your device. Works offline once loaded. Install it to your home screen for the best experience.

Tap any section below to expand it.
🚀 Getting Started (5 minutes)
1. Set up your profile — Tap ⚙️ (top right) → enter your name, title, division, department, and contact info. This appears on your ID card and in all exports.

2. Select your position — In the same Settings screen, choose your role from the "Select Your Position" dropdown. This auto-fills your profile and pre-loads your Roles, Ordinances, and Summary pages.

3. Add your first stop — Go to the STOPS tab, type an address, and tap ADD. The app will geocode it and add it to your list.

4. Optimize your route — Go to the ROUTE tab and tap ⚡ OPTIMIZE. The app uses your GPS location and runs nearest-neighbor + 2-opt + or-opt over real road distances from OSRM (an open-source routing engine), with a barrier-aware fallback when OSRM is offline (knows about the river, Forest Park, the railroad cut, and the irregular grids in far-south and far-north city). A drive time estimate appears after.

5. Clock in — Tap ⏱ IN at the top of the screen when your shift starts. This enables shift tracking, break time, and accurate metrics.
🤖 AI Features (Optional)
Six features unlock when you add an AI key in Settings — Gemini (free) or Anthropic (paid) or both:

· 📷 Photo scan — Photograph a printed route sheet, dispatch list, or whiteboard. AI reads the addresses and adds them to your stop list.
· Address smart-complete — When you type a partial or ambiguous address, AI suggests the most likely full St. Louis address.
· Address list cleanup — When pasting or scanning a batch of addresses, AI normalizes formatting and corrects obvious typos.
· Route narrative — Plain-English description of your optimized route after you tap ⚡ OPTIMIZE.
· Ask Your Data — Natural-language queries against your local stop history (see Ask Your Data section).
· End-of-Day Debrief — AI-generated summary of your day's work (see End-of-Day Debrief section).

Recommended setup: Add a free Gemini key as your primary provider, optionally add an Anthropic key as a paid fallback. By default the app tries Gemini first and falls back to Anthropic only if Gemini errors or rate-limits — most inspectors will never need to pay. You can change the priority (or override per-feature) in Settings → 🎯 AI Provider Priority.

Gemini (free): Sign in to aistudio.google.com/app/apikey with any Google account, tap "Create API key," and copy the AIza… string. Free tier is ~500 requests/day on gemini-2.5-flash — enough for normal inspector use indefinitely. No credit card required. Free-tier prompts may be used by Google to improve their models; for sensitive data, pair with the paid tier or use Anthropic instead.

Anthropic (paid fallback): Visit console.anthropic.com, create an account, prepay $5 in credits (Settings → Billing → Buy credits), then create an API key (Settings → API Keys → Create Key). Anthropic does NOT train on API traffic by default. At Claude Haiku 4.5 vision rates (~$0.004 per photo scan), $5 covers roughly 1,250 scans — about 125 working days at 10 scans/day. No monthly fee, no auto-charge unless you turn on auto-reload yourself.

Note on OpenAI: Not currently compatible with this app's no-server architecture (OpenAI doesn't reliably support direct browser API calls). If that changes, OpenAI support will be added.

Security: Your keys are encrypted on-device with AES-GCM using a PIN as the password (one PIN unlocks all your keys). The encrypted blob lives in this app's private storage — it never leaves your phone unencrypted, and other apps on your phone can't see it.

Models: Gemini calls use gemini-2.5-flash; Anthropic calls use Claude Haiku 4.5. Both are the fast, cheap tier of their respective lineups. The app intentionally won't use more expensive models — this gives you predictable costs and limits damage if your key is ever leaked.

Usage tracking: Settings → 📊 AI Usage shows per-feature call counts and token usage for the current session, so you can see what your keys have spent. Resets when you reload the app. To check your real money balance, go to console.anthropic.com → Billing (Anthropic only — Gemini's free tier doesn't bill).
💬 Ask Your Data
Plain-English queries against your stop history. In the DATA tab, tap 💬 ASK to open a question box. Type a natural-language question and the AI uses your local stop data (never sent verbatim to the model — only aggregate stats and structure) to compose an answer.

Examples that work well:
· "Which neighborhood did I visit most this week?"
· "What's my average dwell time on inspections vs service requests?"
· "How many follow-up flags do I have open?"
· "What time of day am I closing the most stops?"
· "Which addresses have I returned to in the last month?"

What it can't do:
· Won't fabricate data it doesn't have — if you only have one shift logged, "compare last week to this week" returns "not enough data."
· Won't predict future routes or assign work — it analyzes what already happened.
· Won't expose individual address strings to the model in cases where the question is statistical.

Requires an AI key in Settings (Gemini free tier works fine here). Each question is a single AI call (~1¢ per query depending on data size on Anthropic; covered by Gemini's free tier).
⏱ Clock & Shift Metrics
Clock In/Out — Tap ⏱ IN at the start of your shift. The elapsed work time appears below the button. Tap ⏱ OUT to end the shift.

☕ Break — While clocked in, a BREAK button appears. Tap it to pause the work timer. Your break time is tracked separately. Tap RESUME to continue.

Metrics tracked per shift:
· Stops completed and close rate
· Stops per hour and average minutes per stop
· First and last stop times
· Dwell time at stops (from ARRIVE → CLOSE)
· Return visits (same address visited more than once)
· Unique streets covered + top 5 streets by visit count
· Close-rate by day of week (rolling)
· Average shift hours and overtime flag (shift > 8 hours worked)

All shift data is stored locally and included in your exports. Open the DATA tab to view and export.
📊 Data & Exports
Open the DATA tab from the INFO screen to see today's metrics and your shift history.

Date ranges: Today · 7 Days · 30 Days · All Time · Custom.

Export formats:
· CSV Spreadsheet — One row per stop. Columns: #, Inspector, Address, Type, Status, AddedAt, CompletedAt, DurationMins, Lat, Lng, Notes. Opens in Excel or Google Sheets.
· Text Summary — Three-section plain-text report (Field Worker, Manager, Geographic). Good for email.
· Push to Sheets — Sends data directly to a Google Sheet you control. Requires one-time Apps Script setup (see below).

📅 Period Reports — A separate, more detailed report below the main metrics. Pick Day / Week / Month / Year, then use ‹ › to navigate to any period — the current one shows an "● IN PROGRESS" badge and updates live, past ones show "✓ COMPLETED" as a fixed snapshot. Each report includes KPIs plus ward, neighborhood, street, and stop-type breakdowns. Share it as 📋 Copy (plain text to clipboard), 📥 CSV (downloadable spreadsheet), or 🌐 HTML report (a fully-styled standalone page that prints cleanly to PDF from any browser). The ⚙ icon lets you set which day your week starts on.
📧 Email Stop Summaries
What's in a stop summary email? Address, type, times (added / arrived / completed / dwell), GPS coordinates, your inspector name, completion notes, transactions with totals, and the filenames of any photos taken at that stop.

Three ways to send one:
· 📧 EMAIL STOP SUMMARY in the close-stop modal (text only) — opens your share sheet or mail app with the summary pre-filled. Photo filenames are listed in the body so the recipient knows what to look for in the gallery.
· 📧📷 EMAIL SUMMARY + photos attached in the close-stop modal (text + photos) — appears only if photos for this stop are still in the 48-hour cache. Opens the share sheet with the stamped JPEGs pre-attached.
· 📧 EMAIL SUMMARY + PHOTOS in the photo preview modal — uses the photos from the active session you just captured. Always works as long as you haven't tapped Done yet.

Why two versions of the email? Web apps can't read photos from your gallery (browser security rule). The 48-hour cache works around this by keeping in-app copies just long enough to cover the typical email-after-stop window. Past 48 hours the email body still lists every filename — useful so you (or the recipient) can manually attach from the gallery.

Cross-platform compatibility: The summary uses CRLF line endings so it formats correctly in Gmail composer on Android. The mailto: fallback uses an anchor click instead of a navigation, so PWA users on iOS don't lose their session opening Mail.

See Settings → 📧 How email attachments work for the full explainer.
🌅 End-of-Day Debrief
AI-generated summary of your day's work. Tap the 🌅 button in the DATA tab (visible when you have at least 3 completed stops today) to generate a multi-paragraph debrief covering:
· How the day went vs. previous days (time-of-day patterns, completion rate)
· Notable findings — clusters of work in one area, repeat addresses, follow-up flags
· Productivity observations — long dwell times, gaps between stops, overtime
· Anything worth flagging for tomorrow's plan

Use cases:
· Replace the daily handwritten log: copy the debrief into your shift report
· Quick mental wrap-up before you clock out
· Send to a supervisor as an end-of-day status update

The debrief is regeneratable — tap 🌅 again later in the day for an updated version. The AI sees your full local stop log for context but doesn't store anything about your day on Anthropic's servers beyond the standard API call.

Requires Anthropic API key. Uses Claude Haiku 4.5 (cheapest current-generation model). Typical debrief costs a fraction of a cent.
📈 Google Sheets Setup
One-time setup — takes about 5 minutes:

1. Open a new Google Sheet at sheets.google.com
2. Click Extensions → Apps Script
3. Delete the existing code and paste the script below
4. Click Deploy → New deployment → Web app
5. Set "Who has access" to Anyone
6. Click Deploy and copy the Web App URL
7. In this app, tap Export → Push to Sheets → paste the URL

After setup, every time you tap "Push to Sheets" your latest stops and metrics are appended to your sheet automatically. 
function doPost(e) {
  var data = JSON.parse(e.postData.contents);
  var ss = SpreadsheetApp.getActiveSpreadsheet();
  var sh = ss.getSheetByName('Stops') || ss.insertSheet('Stops');
  if (sh.getLastRow() === 0)
    sh.appendRow(['Date','Address','Type','Notes','Done','DurationMins','Lat','Lng','Inspector']);
  (data.stops || []).forEach(s => sh.appendRow([
    data.exportedAt, s.address, s.type,
    s.notes||'', s.done, s.durationMins||'',
    s.lat||'', s.lng||'', data.inspector
  ]));
  return ContentService.createTextOutput('ok');
}
🪪 INFO Tab
ID Card — Your digital business card. Shows your name, title, division, department, scope, email, and phone. Tap it to show a fullscreen version. Upload an ID photo via ⚙️ Settings.

❓ Questions & Follow-Up — Contact information for departments you selected in Settings. CSB is always first. One-tap dialing.

⚖️ Roles & Authority — Three panels (Roles, Summary, Ordinance) loaded from your saved position. Use the position selector to preview any other role without changing your profile. Navigate away and your saved default restores automatically.

📄 Reference Documents — Upload and store images (maps, forms, permit templates, reference sheets) for quick in-field lookup. Stored locally; up to 50 docs.

📋 RESOURCES — Categorized contact list: property & ownership lookup, courts & enforcement records, citation-issuing agencies (SLMPD by district, parking, building, fire, health, state/federal), key city departments, towing, emergency utilities, environmental, GIS tools, animals & wildlife, and local business listings. ~140 entries with one-tap dialing.

📊 Data & Metrics — Your shift data, stop history, and export tools.

❓ Help & Guide — This page.

⚙️ Settings — Includes:
  · Profile (name, title, division, contact info, ID photo)
  · Position selector + Custom Stop Types creator
  · 📷 Stamp Settings (what gets burned into photos)
  · 🗂️ Map Layer Manager (custom GeoJSON layers)
  · Anthropic API Key (encrypted with PIN)
  · 📊 AI Usage (per-feature token counts, this session)
  · 📷 Photo Cache (48h temporary photo storage management)
  · 📧 How email attachments work (explainer)
  · 📈 Google Sheets Sync (one-time setup for push-to-sheets)
  · 💾 Profile Backup & Restore (export/import your full setup as JSON)
  · App Install (PWA add-to-home-screen helper)
  · Share link (send the app URL to a coworker)
📍 Location Metadata & Follow-Up Flag
Neighborhood & ward auto-detection. When a stop is added with a verified address, the app silently looks up which St. Louis neighborhood (1 of 79) and ward the address falls in. The result shows up as a small green line under the address: e.g. 📍 Tower Grove South #15 · Ward 8. Useful for grouping stops by district when you're filing reports or coordinating with ward leaders.

Manual edit. If the auto-lookup is wrong or unavailable (offline first-fetch, etc.), open ✎ Edit Stop from the stop card's utility menu and you can override the neighborhood name, neighborhood number (1–79), and ward number directly. Manual values stick; subsequent auto-lookups won't overwrite them.

Photostamp inclusion. Settings → 📷 Photo Stamp has individual toggles for Neighborhood Name, Neighborhood Number, and Ward Number. Turn any combination on to burn the location info into the stamp on each photo.

🔔 Complete & Flag for Follow-Up. The close-stop modal has two action buttons: the regular ✅ MARK COMPLETE and a ✅🔔 COMPLETE & FLAG FOR FOLLOW-UP. Use the flag variant when a stop technically closes today but you know it'll need another visit (owner agreed to fix by date X, scheduled re-inspection, called CSB about a complaint). The reason field above the buttons is optional — fill it if you want context, leave it blank if you just want the flag.

Where the flag shows up:
· A bright yellow 🔔 banner on the completed stop's card
· A prominent banner at the top of the email summary body
· 🔔 prefix on the email subject line
· A boolean column in the Google Sheets push payload (visible to anyone reviewing the sheet)
· Editable through ✎ Edit Stop — uncheck the flag if no longer needed
🗂️ Map Layers
Tap the 🗂️ icon on the map tab to open the Map Layers panel — toggle visual overlays on top of the route map.

Built-in layers:
· Neighborhoods — all 79 St. Louis neighborhood boundaries with names (labels shorten to just the number when zoomed out, so the boundaries stay readable without disappearing)
· Wards — current city ward boundaries (post-2024 redistricting)
· Police Districts — the 6 SLMPD patrol districts, burnt-orange dashed outline
· City limits — outline of the City of St. Louis

External map resources in the same panel link out to the City's ArcGIS parcel viewer, the official address search, the LRA-owned property map, the Cultural Resources areas-of-review map, the citywide zoning map, the City open data portal, and the Missouri MSDIS portal. These open in your browser.

Custom layers: Settings → Map Layer Manager lets you add your own GeoJSON layers (assignments, district boundaries, target lists, etc.). Custom layers persist across sessions and toggle on/off independently.
🗺️ MAP Tab
Shows your full route as numbered pins on an interactive map plus a polyline trail showing the order. Pinch to zoom, drag to pan.

The trail:
· Solid green line with arrows = stops you've already completed today, in visit order
· Solid blue line tracing actual streets = pending stops, drawn from OSRM road-network geometry when you're online and OSRM mode is selected (Settings → Route Algorithm → Road network)
· Dashed blue line with arrows = pending stops in optimized order, used as a fallback when offline, when you're using the straight-line algorithm, or when the road-network fetch fails
· Direction arrows always sit at midpoints between consecutive stops — they show the logical visit order, not every road bend
· The trail connects: origin → done1 → done2 → ... → pending1 → pending2 → ...
· The transition from your last-done stop to your first-pending stop is part of the pending layer (it's your next move). After completing a stop, the road-network overlay reverts to dashed straight-line until the next Optimize — the geometry is fetched per optimize run.

Pin colors match the stop type — red for Service Requests, blue for Inspections, gold for general — and pending pins show their route number.

Boundary layers (Neighborhoods, Wards) toggle on via the LAYERS button. Neighborhoods now show their official city number after the name (e.g., "Southwest Garden #13") and are drawn with a heavier purple dashed border. Wards are drawn with a thick solid teal border, so the two layers stay distinguishable when both are on.

Tap a pin to open the map popup card. From the popup you can: ▲▼ reorder, 📌 pin as start/end, 🔒 lock position, 📍 mark arrived, 🔍 lookup, 🧭 navigate, and ✓ close. The map redraws after each action with a brief pause so you can see the markers and trail update.

The "you" marker is the blue dot with a halo at your origin. By default this is the city center; tap 📡 in the map header to update it to your current GPS location.

If your stops are geocoded (most are), they appear immediately. Stops added by address alone may take a moment to place while the app looks up coordinates.
✏️ Photo Annotation
Draw on photos before sending. In the photo preview after a stamped capture, tap ✏️ EDIT to enter annotation mode. Tools available:
· Draw — freehand pen with adjustable color and stroke width
· Arrow — drag to draw a labeled arrow pointing at a violation, defect, or feature
· Box — drag to draw a highlight rectangle around an area of interest
· Text — tap to drop a text label anywhere on the photo
· Redact — drag to drop a black rectangle covering license plates, faces, or other PII before the photo gets attached to a public-facing report

Undo/Redo — every annotation step is undoable. The original stamped photo is preserved; annotations are flattened only when you tap Save.

The annotated copy replaces the stamped JPEG in both the phone gallery and the 48-hour cache. Email summaries include the annotated version.

Useful when documenting a violation: arrow to the broken board, text caption "wood rot, west wall", redact the visible street number on the neighbor's house.
📷 Photo Stamping & Cache
What is photostamping? The 📷 button in the top header (and the 📷 button in any stop's Utility menu) opens Prepare Photo — a full-screen setup step, not the camera directly. It shows a live map of parcels and street names near your GPS position, with your nearest 10 addresses listed below as tappable candidates (each with its own 🔍 Property Search button). Tap a candidate to fill the address, use 📍 GPS to reverse-lookup instead, or type it manually. Add optional notes and a tracking number, then tap 📷 Open Camera or 🖼 Pick Existing (choose one or more photos from your gallery — all get stamped with the same address). ⚙ Stamp jumps to Stamp Settings; ✕ Exit backs out without taking a photo.

Per-stop photo sessions: Opening Prepare Photo from a specific stop's Utility menu links the photo to that stop. Each stamped shot increments a 📷 counter on the stop card so you can see at a glance how many photos you've taken at that address.

Filenames are searchable: Stamped photos are named like 2801daltonavenue_042326_101832847.jpg — address, then date (mmddyy), then time (hhmmss) + milliseconds, underscore-separated. You can search this exact name in your Photos / Files app to find any photo later.

What gets burned in — Settings → 📷 Stamp Settings has individual toggles for: address, date, time, inspector name, title/department, GPS coordinates, compass bearing, case number, photo sequence number, neighborhood name, neighborhood number, ward number, and notes. Turn any subset on. Compact form: "Tower Grove South #15 · Ward 8".

Stamp size and position — Choose Small / Medium / Large size and Top / Bottom position in Stamp Settings. Defaults are Medium and Bottom.

Photostamp drift detection — If your phone's clock differs from the network time by more than 30 seconds, the app warns you before stamping. This protects against an inspector accidentally stamping yesterday's date or being off by an hour after a daylight-savings change.

The 48-hour cache: A copy of every stamped photo is also kept inside the app for 48 hours. This is what makes the 📧 Email Summary + photos attached button work for stops you photographed earlier in the day or yesterday. After 48 hours the cached copy is auto-deleted to keep the app small — your phone gallery copies are not affected and remain forever.

Cache size: Capped at 200MB normally, or 50MB on phones with limited free space. When the cache fills up, oldest photos go first; the newest photo for each stop is always preserved.

Manage the cache: Settings → 📷 Photo Cache (48h) shows current size and a "🗑 Clear Cache Now" button. Clearing the cache only affects in-app copies — gallery photos are untouched.

Automatic backup: Stamp Settings has a "✅ Recommended: Auto-backup via Google Photos" card — point Google Photos at your Download folder once (Android) and every future stamped photo backs up automatically, no sign-in inside the app.

Quick mode: Tap the ⚡ toggle in the photo preview to auto-dismiss the preview and reopen the camera after each shot. Good for batch capture (multiple shots at one stop).
💾 Profile Backup & Restore
Export everything as JSON. Settings → 💾 Profile Backup → tap EXPORT BACKUP. A timestamped .json file downloads to your phone containing: your full profile, all custom stop types, Reference Documents, custom map layers, settings, the active stop list, the completed-stop archive, and your shift log. The Anthropic API key is NOT included by design — re-enter it on the new device with your PIN.

Restore on a new device or after a wipe. Settings → 💾 Profile Backup → IMPORT BACKUP → pick the JSON file. The app prompts for confirmation since import overwrites your current data.

What it's good for:
· Getting a new phone — restore in 30 seconds, no re-typing your profile
· Trying a beta build — back up first, restore if anything goes wrong
· Switching between work and personal phones — same profile both places
· Annual archive — keep a snapshot of last year's stop history before clearing the active list

Privacy: The JSON file lives on your phone. Don't email it or store it in cloud services unless you've reviewed the contents — it contains your full address book of visited stops, including any private notes.
🛣️ ROUTE Tab
⚡ Optimize Route Order — Sorts your pending stops using your current GPS location + nearest-neighbor + 2-opt + or-opt improvement passes. Distances come from OSRM (an open-source road-routing engine) so the optimizer follows real streets, not straight-line crow-flies estimates — that's a big deal in a city with the Mississippi, Forest Park, the Mill Creek Valley railroad cut, the interstates, and irregular grids in far-south and far-north city. If OSRM is unreachable (offline or rate-limited) the app falls back to a barrier-aware Haversine model that approximates the same constraints. A drive-time estimate appears after.

📌 Pin start / 📌 Pin end — Lock a stop as your first or last stop regardless of optimization. Tap 📌 in the route card or in the map popup. Useful when you have a scheduled appointment at a specific address.

🔒 Lock position (waypoint lock) — Lock a specific stop at a specific position number in the route. The optimizer keeps that stop at that index and reroutes the surrounding stops around it. You can lock multiple stops; the optimizer respects all of them simultaneously. Tap 🔒 again on a locked stop to unlock.

↺ Reset Order — Returns stops to the order they were added.

▲▼ Reorder — Tap the arrows on any route card to manually bump a stop up or down in priority.

Route AI Narrative — After optimizing, if you have an Anthropic API key set, the app generates a brief plain-English description of the route sequence (e.g., "Start downtown, swing through Tower Grove, finish in Carondelet"). Cheap text-only call.

Navigate all — Tap 🗺️ in the route tab header to open the full multi-stop route in Google Maps (up to 10 waypoints).
📋 STOPS Tab
Adding stops:
· Type an address in the input field and tap ADD (or press Enter)
· 🎤 Voice — speak addresses one at a time; pause between each
· 📷 Photo scan — photograph a printed route sheet; AI reads the addresses (requires an AI key — Gemini or Anthropic — in Settings)
· 📋 Paste — copy a list of addresses from any source and paste it
· 📊 Spreadsheet — upload a CSV or Excel file with addresses in the first column

Stop types: Tap the color badge on any stop to cycle through Inspection · Service Request · Work Order · General. You can also create your own custom types with custom colors and icons via Settings → Custom Stop Types.

Stop buttons (left to right):
· 📝 Notes — add or view field notes for this stop
· 🔍 Property search — copies the address and opens the City's official address-search page
· CSB — copies address and opens the Citizens Service Bureau request form
· 📍 ARRIVE — tap when you pull up to the stop; starts a dwell timer
· ✓ CLOSE — mark the stop complete; prompts for completion notes and offers email options (see Email Stop Summaries below)
· 🧭 (round, right) — navigate to this address via Google Maps

Photo capture per stop: Open a stop's Utility menu (⋯) and tap 📷 to start a photo session linked to that stop — this takes you through Prepare Photo first (see Photo Stamping & Cache below), then the camera. The header's 📷 button works the same way but isn't linked to any stop. Each stamped photo is saved to your phone gallery AND cached in the app for 48 hours so you can email them with the stop summary later.

Completed stops collapse to a separate "Completed" section at the bottom. Use ↩ to restore or 🗑 to delete permanently.

Duplicate detection: Adding an address already on the list shows a prompt — tap to confirm or cancel.
💡 Tips & Best Practices
· Clock in before adding stops so your shift metrics are accurate from the first address.
· Tap 📍 ARRIVE when you pull up to a stop — this starts a dwell timer so you know how long each visit took.
· Add notes at close — the CLOSE dialog has a notes field. Use it for condition observations, owner contact attempts, or case numbers.
· Pin your first stop on the ROUTE tab if you know you need to start at a specific address (e.g., a scheduled appointment). Pin your last stop if you want to end near home or near a specific neighborhood for the next day's prep.
· Check the trail on the MAP tab mid-shift — the green line shows where you've been, the dashed blue shows what's left. Helpful for visualizing whether the optimizer's order still makes sense as the day progresses.
· Email summaries with photos work for 48 hours. If you took photos at a stop today and want to email them tomorrow morning, the cached copies are still in the app — just open the stop and use the 📧📷 button. After 48 hours, the email body still lists every photo filename so you can attach manually from your gallery.
· Run quick mode for batch photos at multi-photo stops — toggle ⚡ in the photo preview and the camera reopens automatically after each shot.
· Annotate before sending. The ✏️ EDIT tool in the photo preview lets you arrow-and-label violations, redact PII, and write captions — much clearer for whoever's reading the report.
· Backup before risky changes. Settings → 💾 Profile Backup → EXPORT BACKUP. Takes 5 seconds. Saves everything.
· Install the app to your home screen via Settings → App Install for offline access and a faster experience.
· The map auto-updates when you switch to it after making changes — no need to refresh.
· Completed stops save permanently until you clear them. Your history is available under "All Time" in the DATA tab.
· Watch your AI spend in Settings → 📊 AI Usage if you're using photo scan heavily — image inputs are the most expensive type of request (~$0.004 each on Claude Haiku 4.5 vision). Check your real money balance at console.anthropic.com → Billing.
· Photo gallery is the permanent record. The app's 48-hour cache is convenience only. Photos in your phone's Photos / Downloads folder never expire.
🔧 Toolbar Buttons
These appear at the top of every screen:

· ⚖️ CASE — Opens Missouri Case.net (22nd Judicial Circuit) for municipal court case lookups
· 📞 CSB — Opens the Citizens Service Bureau. Copies the current stop's address to clipboard first if one is active
· 🔍 Search — City of St. Louis property lookup by address
· 📷 Photostamp — Opens your camera with stop-aware overlay. Stamped photos save to your gallery and to the 48-hour cache (see Photo Stamping & Cache section)
· ⚙️ (top right) — Opens your Settings / profile editor

✨ Supercharge with AI — Appears as a small link in the stop-count bar only when no API key is set. Opens the Supercharge modal that walks you through getting an Anthropic key and the real cost (~$0.004 per photo scan, $5 lasts ~1,250 scans). Once a key is saved, the link disappears.

Other resources (city departments, wildlife rescue, pest control, property lookup tools) are available in the Info tab under the 📋 RESOURCES button.
📬 Contact & Support
Questions, feedback, bug reports, or partnership inquiries — reach us directly.
✉️ Email Support
support@yourcityroute.com

Notes are saved to this stop and included in exports.

📡 Location Unavailable
GPS requires either HTTPS or being saved as a PWA. Here are your options:
📍 FALLBACK: IP Location (already tried)
City-block accuracy via your IP address.
Good enough for route planning when you're at your desk.
Not suitable for real-time field tracking.
⌨️ ALWAYS WORKS: Manual Entry
Type your starting address in the Route tab.
Works from any file or URL.
📷 Import Addresses
📋 How to Import Addresses
Change Stop Type
—
💰 Record Payment
—
1. OPEN PAYMENT APP (OPTIONAL)
Tap an app to launch it for the transaction. Come back after payment completes to log it.
2. LOG THE TRANSACTION
💳 Payment App Setup
Toggle apps you accept payments through, and enter your handle/username so the "OPEN APP" button pre-fills the recipient.
📷 Prepare Photo
Set the details that will be stamped onto this photo. You can edit them again after the shot.
Tip: tap Pick Existing and select multiple photos to stamp all of them with the same address.
📡 Getting your location…
📷
Stamping photo…
📷 Stamped Photo
✎ Edit stamp (this shot only)
💾 Download saves to your phone's Downloads. 📤 Share opens the share sheet (email, Drive, Files, Photos, etc.). 💾📤 Save & Share does both in one tap — saves to Downloads, then opens the share sheet so you can email or send it.
Opens your share sheet with the stop summary and the photos from this session attached. Works only while this preview is open — once you tap Done, the photos stay in your gallery but can\u2019t be re-attached from inside the app.
📷 Stamp Settings
Choose what gets burned into the corner of every stamped photo.
Stamp size
How large the stamp text appears on your photo.
Stamp position
Where the stamp bar appears. Switch to top if your subjects are usually at the bottom of the frame.
✅ Recommended: Auto-backup via Google Photos
Stamped photos already save to your phone's Download folder. Point Google Photos at that folder once and every future photo backs up automatically — no sign-in flow inside this app, no setup screen to fight with.

Android — Google Photos app → profile icon (top right) → Photos settings → Backup → Back up device folders → turn on Download.

iPhone — already covered: this app's "Save All" step already saves stamped photos to your Camera Roll, and iOS Google Photos backs that up automatically once Backup is turned on.

Either way, your photos then show up at photos.google.com on any desktop/laptop — that's the "get them onto my hard drive without a cable" part, done. Same idea works with OneDrive or Dropbox's own device-folder-backup settings if you'd rather use one of those instead.
✎ Edit Stop
Change the address, stop type, or initial notes on this stop.
📍 LOCATION METADATA
Auto-filled from STL GIS once the address has coordinates. Edit manually if the lookup is wrong or unavailable.
＋ Create Custom Stop Type
Add a custom category to the stop type dropdown. Delete or edit any time from Settings.
🗺️ Map Layers
Toggle layers on or off. Custom layers from a list of addresses can be added below.
🔥 CHRONIC PHOTO ACTIVITY
Paste addresses · Upload CSV · Scan a printed list
🗺️ OFFICIAL CITY MAPS
🧭 Survey Mode
Scope your work to one neighborhood, track your GPS breadcrumb trail, and check off streets and alleys as you cover them.
🔥 Chronic Photo Activity
Every saved PhotoStamp photo is tagged to its address (or GPS spot if no address). Locations that keep coming up show as colored markers on the map — set how many photos, over what window, counts as a pattern.
📄 Coverage & Activity Report
Sessions, coverage checklist, chronic locations, and the photo log — everything tracked for one neighborhood, pulled into one exportable report.

📋 Resources
❤ Patreon
Tap any link to open · Phone numbers dial directly
This app is funded by local government sponsorships and listings on this page — no pop-up ads or banners. Just the information you need, where you know to look.
📣 List Your Business in Resources
Reach city field workers exactly when they need your service
Who sees your listing
STLCityRoute is used daily by City of St. Louis field inspectors — nuisance, building, housing, animal control, zoning, and environmental health. Your listing appears in the Resources panel, one tap away at the exact moment a worker needs to refer your type of service.
🌱
Nonprofits list free
Qualified 501(c)(3) nonprofits providing services relevant to city field work receive a complimentary Standard listing. Contact us with your EIN to apply. Subject to availability and annual review.
⭐ PREMIERE
Premiere Listing
Top of Resources page + top of category · alphabetical among Premiere
$150/yr
Your listing appears prominently at the top of the entire Resources page AND at the top of your category — above all Featured and Standard listings. Multiple Premiere listings are sorted alphabetically. Includes name, phone, website, and extended two-line description.
FEATURED
Featured Listing
Top of category (below Premiere) · alphabetical among Featured
$75/yr
Appears at the top of your category section, above all Standard listings and below any Premiere listings. Sorted alphabetically among other Featured businesses. Includes name, phone, website, and one-line description.
Standard Listing
Listed in category · alphabetical among Standard
$25/yr
Listed in your category, sorted alphabetically among Standard listings. Includes business name, phone number, website, and one-line description. One-tap dial for field workers.
ALL LISTINGS INCLUDE
📞 One-tap phone dial
🔗 Website link
📧 Up to 4 contacts
✏️ Free info updates
OPEN CATEGORIES
Pest ControlRodent, insect, vermin
Wildlife RemovalLicensed nuisance trapping
Property LawCode enforcement defense
Towing & RecoveryAbandoned vehicles
EnvironmentalAsbestos, mold, hazmat
Boarding & SecuringVacant structures
DemolitionLicensed demo contractors
Property RehabDistressed property work
Policy
St. Louis area businesses with services relevant to city field work. All listings reviewed before publication. Listings include: business name (linked to website), up to 10-word description, and up to 4 email/phone contacts. Advertising never influences official city contacts — paid listings appear only in the Local Business Listings section. Annual billing · invoice or Venmo · live within 48 hours of payment · lapsed listings removed and slot re-opens.
Ready to list your business?
Email us your business name, category, and preferred tier. We'll confirm availability and get you live within 48 hours.
📧 support@yourcityroute.com

Route optimization and the map need your location. Takes under 2 minutes.
